problem.title: H-Index
problem.description: Given an array of integers citations where citations[i] is the number of citations a researcher received for their ith paper, return the researcher's h-index.
assumptions: h-index: largest h such that h papers have ≥h citations each.

example.explanation: h=3: researcher has 3 papers with ≥3 citations. Papers: 6,5,3 ≥ 3; but 4 papers don't all have ≥4 cites.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Sorting
solutions.description: Sort the citations array in ascending order, then scan from the front: for each paper at index i the remaining papers (n - i) have at least citations[i] citations. The first position where citations[i] >= n - i gives the h-index directly as n - i. The key insight is that after sorting, n - i is always the count of papers with citations at least as large as the current one.
